events.label: assistant
events.text:
Wrote the compliance assessment to `/app/answer.json`.

**Verdict: fail (high risk).** A backstabbed (friction push-in) termination is being used on a 20A branch circuit. Standard push-in backstab terminals are only listed for 14 AWG conductors on 15A circuits — using one with a 12 AWG/20A circuit exceeds the terminal's listing (NEC 110.3(B), 110.14(A)) and creates a loose, high-resistance connection prone to overheating/arcing under sustained load. That's exactly why journeymen avoid the practice even though it can slip past a cursory inspection ("legal" in a narrow sense but not code-compliant once you apply the listing requirement). Corrective action: re-terminate on screw terminals or a listed 20A clamp-type back-wire device, and re-inspect before closeout.
